faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Teaneck, NJ?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Teaneck typically ranges from $350 to $2,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, property size, and number of colonies present. A standard single-family home treatment averages $500–$800, while larger properties or severe infestations with multiple nests may require more extensive treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes for all Teaneck residents.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my Teaneck home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include piles of sawdust (frass) near wooden structures, rustling sounds inside walls, and sightings of large black ants especially during spring evenings. Carpenter ants in Teaneck are most active from March through October and are often spotted near windows, doors, and moisture-prone areas like bathrooms and kitchens. Winged carpenter ants emerging indoors indicate an established colony within your structure.

faqs.question: How quickly can you treat carpenter ants in Teaneck?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service for Teaneck residents in most cases. Initial treatment typically takes 1–3 hours depending on property size and infestation extent. Most infestations show significant reduction within 7–14 days, with complete colony elimination usually achieved within 30–60 days through our comprehensive treatment and follow-up program.

faqs.question: Are carpenter ants common in Teaneck, NJ?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants are very common in Teaneck due to the township's mature tree canopy, older housing stock, and humid climate near the Hackensack River valley. Teaneck's many wooden structures, including decks, porches, and older homes with wood siding, provide ideal nesting sites. Peak activity occurs in spring and early summer when colonies expand and winged ants swarm to establish new nests.
